markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement search across titles, block text, tags, source kinds, symbols, WPs, MTs, and UserManual pages. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: GraphSearchAndFilters leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: GraphSearchAndFilters is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Implemented MT-186 backend graph search over Loom blocks, ProjectKnowledgeIndex entities, and UserManual pages while preserving the legacy block-only /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search API. The heterogeneous surface is explicit at /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search and storage.search_loom_graph.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - real PostgreSQL graph search across loom_blocks, knowledge_entities, user_manual_pages, and user_manual_sections; literal LIKE escaping; offset-aware source prefetch; block-scoped filter handling

src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs - legacy /loom/search restored to block-only; new /loom/graph-search route added for heterogeneous graph search; existing LoomSearchExecuted telemetry preserved

src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loom_graph_search_filters_tests.rs - fail-closed real-PostgreSQL MT-186 proof for heterogeneous hits, source_kind filtering, literal wildcard behavior, offset pagination, and block-filter leakage prevention
evidence.compatibility_shim: Existing block-only storage search and /loom/search response shape remain intact. Heterogeneous non-block hits are only returned by search_loom_graph or /loom/graph-search.

evidence.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test loom_graph_search_filters_tests -- --nocapture && cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils,duckdb-flight-recorder --lib loom_search_backend_tier -- --nocapture && cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils,duckdb-flight-recorder --lib view_and_search_emit_events -- --nocapture && cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test loom_pins_views_tests favorite_flag_survives_search_results -- --nocapture && rustfmt --edition 2021 --check --config skip_children=true <MT-186 Rust files>
evidence.result: PASS: loom_graph_search_filters_tests 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored, 0 skipped; API loom_search_backend_tier passed; API view_and_search_emit_events passed and asserts legacy /loom/search remains block-only; favorite_flag_survives_search_results passed; targeted rustfmt check passed.
evidence.postgre_sql_evidence: The MT-186 integration target uses knowledge_pg_support against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL and now panics if PostgreSQL is unavailable, so a skip cannot count as proof.

evidence.summary: Raman initial review returned FAIL for three blocking gaps: legacy /loom/search response compatibility, merged pagination, and block-scoped filter leakage into non-block graph hits. All findings were fixed with code changes and final proof commands passed.
findings_closed: /loom/search restored to Vec<LoomBlockSearchResult>; /loom/graph-search added for heterogeneous graph results.
search_loom_graph now fetches offset+limit rows per eligible source before merged pagination.
Block-scoped filters such as tag_ids, mention_ids, content_type, mime, and backlink_depth no longer return knowledge/UserManual hits that cannot satisfy those filters.
MT-186 real-PostgreSQL test now fails closed instead of silently passing without PostgreSQL.

evidence.summary: No frontend GUI surface was changed for MT-186. The current closure is backend/storage/API graph search; validator-readable evidence is the real-PostgreSQL integration target plus API telemetry tests. Future UI consumption can bind to /loom/graph-search without changing the legacy /loom/search contract.
KB ready checklist PASS emitted before READY_FOR_VALIDATION handoff.

findings: Initial FAIL: /loom/search returned heterogeneous graph results by default and could break block-only clients.
Initial FAIL: search_loom_graph applied offset after fetching only limit rows per source.
Initial FAIL: block-scoped filters did not constrain knowledge/UserManual graph hits.
Initial MEDIUM: MT-186 PostgreSQL proof could silently pass when PostgreSQL was unavailable.
fixes: Restored legacy /loom/search to block-only and added explicit /loom/graph-search for heterogeneous results.
Prefetch offset+limit rows per source before merged pagination.
Treat block-scoped filters as block-only until other source kinds can satisfy them.
Changed MT-186 integration tests to panic when real PostgreSQL is unavailable.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-186 requires Handshake-native Loom graph search across titles, block text, tags, source kinds, symbols, WPs, MTs, and UserManual pages over PostgreSQL authority. The code delivers a real, complete search_loom_graph implementation querying real PG authority tables, wired to a live Axum route, with a real-PostgreSQL integration test suite that fails closed when PG is absent. No stubs, mocks, or placeholders on the required path.
findings: storage/postgres.rs:7454 search_loom_graph is a real heterogeneous search over real PG authority tables: loom_blocks (via search_loom_blocks), knowledge_entities for symbol/work_packet/micro_task (7544-7637), knowledge_rich_documents for documents (7642-7748), user_manual_pages + user_manual_sections (7753-7884), and knowledge_wiki_projections (7889-7962). Real columns selected, real fetch_all against self.pool.

Literal-wildcard safety is real: escape_like_token + ESCAPE '\\' used on every ILIKE branch (e.g. postgres.rs:7576-7585, 7677-7694), and the test mt186_graph_search_...with_filters asserts a '%' query returns empty (tests file:299-307). Empty/whitespace query short-circuits to Vec::new() (7468-7470).

Block-scoped filter leak prevention is real: loom_search_has_block_scoped_filters gates knowledge/document/usermanual/wiki branches (7541, 7639-7640, 7750-7751, 7886-7887); test mt186_block_scoped_filters_do_not_leak_non_block_hits (tests:970-1071) proves a tag filter excludes symbols/usermanual and returns only the tagged block.

Offset/limit pagination prefetches offset+source_limit per source before merged skip/take (7477-7484, 7976-7980); test mt186_graph_search_offsets_after_prefetching_each_source (tests:929-967) proves no row drop at offset 10 limit 5.

API route is real and wired: api/loom.rs:287 registers /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search -> search_loom_graph handler (3502) calling state.storage.search_loom_graph (3539) and emitting a real LoomSearchExecuted FlightRecorder/EventLedger event (3549). Legacy /loom/search restored to block-only handler search_loom_blocks (api/loom.rs:278-279, 3388), satisfying the adversarial-review compatibility requirement.

Proof touches real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs resolves POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL or ManagedPostgres::ensure_running; the pg_or_skip! macro panics ('requires real PostgreSQL') when none is available, so a skip cannot masquerade as PASS (tests:24-33).
No deferred-live escapes: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/LiveClientUnavailable across the search code returned no hits on the required path.
Self-cert clean: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260614-200747 set status READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not COMPLETED); the COMPLETED transition is left to the validator role.

Non-blocking aside (paperwork only, not a fail reason): the evidence_refs test logs under ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/mt186/ are not present on this disk, and the packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194. Both are expected wind-down paperwork drift and do not affect the code verdict.
evidence_checked: git log: MT-186 WP-009 work landed in checkpoint commit c49afb7f

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
